summary.what: Warn loudly when the post-rewrite hook relinks ledger entries after a rebase
summary.why: The hook remapped SHAs silently, leaving an uncommitted relink; if already pushed, origin kept an orphaned anchor and the rewritten commit read as pending for fresh clones — the exact failure this session hit
summary.how: Track remapped files in the hook and emit a stderr warning when any change; added a test that runs the hook and asserts remap + warning + silence on no match

notes: Chose warn-loudly over auto-commit deliberately: the hook fires mid-rebase / mid-pull --rebase, and injecting a commit into a git flow the user is driving is intrusive and surprising. Warning keeps the tool honest without seizing control of history. Two shell-in-Go-string lint quirks surfaced (dupword on nested-loop done/done, gosec+noctx on the test exec) — resolved with inline loop comments and CommandContext rather than blanket nolint.
